page: Microsoft Azure E32-8as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor runs at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for read-only operations on both small and large blocks, but write operations on large uncached blocks are weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, perform at average levels.

With 256.0 GB of RAM allocated to 8 vCPUs, this instance provides high memory density relative to its compute resources. This profile is suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require proportional CPU scaling, such as medium-sized in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound data processing. However, the lack of local storage and the weak large-block memory write bandwidth represent key tradeoffs for write-heavy storage operations. It is best suited for relational databases, caching systems like Redis, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-8as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine configured with an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores and 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance alongside average multi-core and memory read performance, though large-block memory write bandwidth is weak. This high memory density relative to compute resources makes the instance cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require extensive CPU scaling. It is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8as_v5 is an EASv5 virtual machine with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and dedicated AMD EPYC processors for memory-bound enterprise workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
No bundled local storage, but compatible with Premium Storage
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications
